data.body:
## Acceptance Criteria

## Testing Mode
GREEN

AC added via sprint orchestrator (original ticket had no AC section).

Files to split: analyze-tool-use.py (1,225 lines), ticket-graph.py (1,152 lines), bridge-inbound.py (1,148 lines), bridge-outbound.py (1,053 lines).

Note: bridge-inbound.py and bridge-outbound.py also have handler extraction tasks (5ee9-8591, 9f0d-860d). Coordinate with those tasks — split at module level first, then handler extraction tasks extract functions from within each module.

- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check passes (exit 0)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py 2>/dev/null || true
- [ ] analyze-tool-use.py is under 1000 lines
  Verify: wc -l $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/analyze-tool-use.py | awk '$1 < 1000'
- [ ] ticket-graph.py is under 1000 lines
  Verify: wc -l $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-graph.py | awk '$1 < 1000'
- [ ] bridge-inbound.py is under 1000 lines
  Verify: wc -l $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-inbound.py | awk '$1 < 1000'
- [ ] bridge-outbound.py is under 1000 lines
  Verify: wc -l $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/scripts/bridge-outbound.py | awk '$1 < 1000'
- [ ] All new module files are importable (no broken imports)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -c "import importlib.util; print('import check: OK')"
- [ ] Original entry point files re-export all public symbols (backward compat)
  Verify: cd $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && python3 -c "import plugins.dso.scripts.bridge_inbound" 2>/dev/null || python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0, 'plugins/dso/scripts'); import importlib; print('entry point re-exports OK')"
